title: PostgreSQL 18 Schema, Constraints, Keys, and Query-Driven Indexes · Changed-Schema Lab
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers postgresql 18 schema, constraints, keys, and query-driven indexes into a changed constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of constrained TypeScript feed-service relational model with automated and judgment…
objectives: Model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership.
Use stable internal identifiers plus tenant-scoped external identities and preserve URL moves, aliases, and provenance.
Encode required fields, lengths, checks, states, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, and temporal rules in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Relational Domain
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 9043 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile relational entities, stable keys, constraints, tenant scope, and query-driven indexes; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Relational Domain.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model tenants, principals, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, versions, people, categories, fetches, leases, and reader state with explicit ownership. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Relational Domain, keep Feed Ts Relational Domain connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
